ofno11k.text: Great job bro, definitely useful for branding and visual identity!

ofpblka.text: From my personal testing, as long as the barcode is large enough, it scans easily provided the bottom horizontal unmodified section remains intact. Most things should work fine, though certain shapes might struggle at very small dimensions. Wackier styles might require larger dimensions to ensure easy scanning. Thanks for commenting :)
ofpblka.parent_text: Cool concept. Curious how far the stylization can go before scanners start choking on them — is there a rough "visual distortion budget" you stay under, or do the heavier styles just get tested device-by-device?

oghvzwo.parent_text: This is so cool!! Never even thought about trying to change a bar code.

ofqyt7f.text: They kept it scannable by always putting a solid bar at the bottom. Seems pretty trivial to me. (Not to downplay OP's work. Looks like a good tool.)

oiomf0p.text: Please secure your intellectual property rights. Like right now if you haven't. At the very least, register your code. Gather your UI and graphics, too and copyright in US and UK at the very least. Look up IP law. Do what you want with it but protect yourself. Really cool idea and the execution's great too.
